← Back to All Roadmaps
GRC Practitioner Roadmap · 2025 Edition

GOVERNANCE
RISK &
Compliance

A practitioner-built guide for transitioning into Cloud GRC roles — covering frameworks, tools, audit skills, and everything you need to compete for positions at consultancies, enterprises, and regulators.

12–18 months to job-ready5 progressive phasesAWS · Azure · GCPISO 27001 · SOC 2 · NIST · PCI DSS · GDPRMultiple career paths
GRC
Your entry point to
cloud security leadership
Learning Phases
01 / 06
Phase 01GRC &
Security Foundations0 – 2 Months

Before anything else, build your mental model of why GRC exists. Understand the language of risk and compliance — these are the words auditors, regulators, and boards speak. You don't need to be deeply technical yet, but you do need to understand how technology creates risk.

Core GRC Concepts
  • Governance: who owns decisions and accountability
  • Risk management: identify, assess, treat, monitor
  • Compliance: obligations, controls, evidence
  • The three lines of defence model
  • Control types: preventive, detective, corrective
  • Risk appetite vs risk tolerance
Security Fundamentals
  • CIA Triad — your anchor for everything
  • Threats vs vulnerabilities vs risks
  • Authentication, authorisation, identity basics
  • Encryption at rest and in transit (conceptual)
  • Patch management and vulnerability basics
  • Incident response lifecycle
Business & Legal Context
  • How organisations structure IT and security teams
  • Board-level cyber reporting
  • Data protection law basics: GDPR overview
  • Contracts: DPAs, SLAs, MSAs — what they mean
  • Vendor risk management fundamentals
Cloud Awareness
  • What is cloud? IaaS vs PaaS vs SaaS
  • Shared responsibility model (memorise this)
  • Why cloud changes the compliance landscape
  • Data residency and sovereignty concepts
  • Multi-cloud vs hybrid environments
CompTIA Security+ (validates security fundamentals)Google Cybersecurity CertificateISACA IT Fundamentals Certificate
Phase 02Frameworks &
Standards Deep Dive2 – 5 Months

Frameworks are your primary toolkit. You'll spend much of your GRC career living inside these documents. Don't just skim them — read the actual standards, understand the intent behind controls, and start mentally mapping them against real cloud environments. This is what separates a great GRC practitioner from a checkbox monkey.

ISO 27001 / 27002
  • ISMS scope, context, interested parties (Clause 4)
  • Leadership and risk treatment (Clauses 5–8)
  • Annex A controls — all 93, know the key ones cold
  • Statement of Applicability (SoA) writing
  • Certification audit process: Stage 1, Stage 2
  • Continual improvement (Clause 10) and NCRs
NIST CSF & SP 800-53
  • CSF 2.0: Govern, Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover
  • Current Profiles vs Target Profiles
  • SP 800-53 control families (AC, AU, CM, IA, SC, SI)
  • FedRAMP: US federal cloud compliance
  • NIST RMF: 7-step risk management framework
SOC 2 (Type I & II)
  • Trust Services Criteria (Security, Availability, Confidentiality, Privacy, PI)
  • Type I vs Type II — what organisations actually need
  • Common Criteria (CC) control mapping
  • Evidence collection and control testing
  • Vendor management from a SOC 2 perspective
PCI DSS v4.0
  • Cardholder Data Environment (CDE) scoping
  • 12 PCI requirements — know the structure
  • SAQs vs QSA engagements
  • Network segmentation to reduce scope
  • Cloud tokenisation and encryption strategies
GDPR & Privacy
  • 6 lawful bases for processing personal data
  • Data subject rights (access, erasure, portability)
  • Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs)
  • Records of Processing Activities (RoPA)
  • Cross-border transfer mechanisms (SCCs, BCRs)
  • Breach notification: 72-hour rule
Sector-Specific Frameworks
  • HIPAA (healthcare): PHI safeguards
  • Cyber Essentials / CE+ (UK government supply chain)
  • DORA (EU financial sector resilience)
  • NIS2 Directive (EU critical infrastructure)
  • CIS Controls v8 (practical hardening)
ISO 27001 Lead Implementer (PECB or BSI)CISA — Certified Information Systems AuditorISO 27001 Lead AuditorISACA CRISC (Risk and Controls)
Phase 03Cloud GRC
in Practice5 – 9 Months

Now bridge frameworks to cloud reality. Cloud environments challenge traditional compliance assumptions — controls that work on-premises need re-thinking when infrastructure is ephemeral, multi-tenant, and API-driven. This phase is where you become genuinely valuable.

Cloud Risk Assessments
  • Cloud-specific threat modelling (data exposure, IAM abuse, misconfiguration)
  • Inherent vs residual risk in cloud contexts
  • Third-party cloud risk: assess your SaaS vendors
  • TPRM (Third-Party Risk Management) workflows
  • Risk registers: format, ownership, review cadence
  • Cloud asset inventory and classification
Cloud-Native Controls
  • AWS: Config Rules, SCPs, CloudTrail, Security Hub
  • Azure: Policy, Blueprints, Defender for Cloud
  • GCP: Org Policy, Security Command Center
  • CSPM tools: Wiz, Prisma Cloud, Orca — learn one deeply
  • Mapping CSPM findings to framework controls
  • Evidence extraction from cloud-native tooling
Audit & Evidence Collection
  • Control testing: design vs operating effectiveness
  • Evidence quality: what auditors actually want
  • Automated evidence collection (Drata, Vanta, Secureframe)
  • GRC platforms: ServiceNow GRC, RSA Archer, OneTrust
  • Walkthrough interviews: preparing technical teams
  • Audit trail: CloudTrail logs as audit evidence
Policy & Documentation
  • Policy hierarchy: policies → standards → procedures → guidelines
  • Writing for different audiences: technical vs board
  • Cloud security policy templates (IS, access, encryption)
  • Acceptable use and data classification policies
  • Policy review cycles and version control
Continuous Compliance
  • Compliance-as-code: policy enforcement via IaC
  • Automated benchmark scanning: Prowler, ScoutSuite
  • Drift detection: when cloud config deviates from policy
  • Control monitoring dashboards and KRIs
  • Exceptions management process
Data Governance in Cloud
  • Data classification schemes (Public, Internal, Confidential, Secret)
  • Data lineage and flow mapping (DFDs)
  • DLP controls: AWS Macie, Azure Purview, Google DLP
  • Data retention and destruction policies
  • Cloud data sovereignty: where is data stored and processed
AWS Cloud Practitioner (cloud fluency baseline)AZ-900 Azure FundamentalsCCSP — Certified Cloud Security ProfessionalAWS Security Specialty (stretch goal)
Phase 04Advanced GRC
& Specialisation9 – 13 Months

This phase develops the higher-order GRC skills that take you from analyst to manager, consultant, or specialist. Pick 1–2 domains to go deep on. The ability to run a risk committee, present to a board, or lead a gap assessment will set you apart.

Risk Quantification
  • FAIR model: Factor Analysis of Information Risk
  • Qualitative vs quantitative risk scoring
  • Expected Loss (LEF × LM) calculations
  • Risk heat maps and registers at enterprise scale
  • Communicating risk in financial terms to leadership
Vendor & Supply Chain Risk
  • Vendor tiering: critical, high, medium, low
  • Due diligence questionnaires (VSQs, SIG Lite, CAIQ)
  • Reviewing vendor SOC 2 reports: what to look for
  • Contractual security requirements and SLAs
  • Cloud provider contractual terms (AWS, Azure, Google BAAs)
  • Offboarding and data deletion assurance
Business Continuity & DR
  • BIA (Business Impact Analysis) methodology
  • RTO and RPO: definitions and how to derive them
  • BCM lifecycle: ISO 22301 overview
  • Cloud DR strategies: backup, warm standby, multi-region
  • Tabletop exercises and DR test documentation
GRC Programme Management
  • Building and running a GRC function from scratch
  • KPIs and KRIs for the security programme
  • Board reporting: the CISO dashboard
  • Stakeholder management and security culture
  • Budget justification for security investments
Regulatory Intelligence
  • Regulatory change management: monitoring new obligations
  • Multi-jurisdictional compliance mapping
  • Regulator engagement and examination readiness
  • Working with legal and DPO functions
  • GDPR supervisory authority liaison
GRC Tooling Mastery
  • Drata / Vanta / Secureframe: automated compliance platforms
  • ServiceNow GRC (enterprise) — basic workflow understanding
  • OneTrust for privacy and third-party risk
  • Jira / Confluence for control tracking and documentation
  • Power BI / Tableau for risk reporting dashboards
CISM — Certified Information Security ManagerCCSP — Certified Cloud Security ProfessionalCGEIT — Governance of Enterprise ITCRISC — Risk and Information Systems ControlCDPSE — Data Privacy Solutions EngineerBCI Certificate in Business Continuity
Phase 05Job Ready &
Portfolio Built13 – 18 Months

GRC roles demand evidence of process thinking, not just theoretical knowledge. Build artefacts that demonstrate you can actually do the job. Your portfolio should show a gap assessment, a risk register, a policy document, and ideally a mock audit report. These become your interview talking points.

Portfolio Artefacts to Build
  • ISO 27001 gap assessment for a fictitious cloud SaaS company
  • SOC 2 readiness checklist with cloud control mappings
  • Risk register template with 10+ realistic cloud risks assessed
  • Cloud security policy pack (IS policy, access control, encryption)
  • DPIA template for a cloud-hosted HR system
  • Mock board-level risk report (1-pager format)
Interview Preparation
  • "Walk me through an ISO 27001 implementation you'd lead"
  • "How would you scope a SOC 2 for a cloud-native startup?"
  • "How do you explain a high risk to a board that wants to accept it?"
  • "What controls map to GDPR Article 32?"
  • "We have a new AWS account — what's your first 30 days?"
  • Know your cert content cold — interviewers will probe
Visibility & Network
  • LinkedIn: document your framework learning journey
  • Publish a "GRC in AWS" or "ISO 27001 for startups" blog post
  • Join ISACA, (ISC)², IAPP chapters in your region
  • Attend BSides, ISACA conferences, CSA events
  • Volunteer: help a small charity or NGO with their GRC basics
Key Compliance Frameworks
02 / 06
Mandatory Know
ISO 27001
International Standard for Information Security Management Systems
The global gold standard. Requires building a formal ISMS with documented risk treatment. Popular in UK, EU, and global enterprise supply chains. Certification requires a third-party audit by an accredited body.
When you'll use it: Any enterprise GRC role, consultancy, supply chain compliance, vendor assessments
Mandatory Know
SOC 2
System and Organisation Controls — Trust Services Criteria
US-origin but globally adopted for SaaS and cloud providers. Customers demand SOC 2 Type II reports as proof of controls. Uses Trust Services Criteria: Security, Availability, Confidentiality, Privacy, Processing Integrity.
When you'll use it: Tech companies, SaaS startups, any org serving US enterprise customers
Mandatory Know
GDPR
General Data Protection Regulation (EU/UK)
Non-negotiable for any org handling EU/UK personal data. Fines up to 4% global turnover. Requires lawful basis for processing, privacy by design, breach reporting within 72 hours, and DPIAs for high-risk processing.
When you'll use it: Every role in the UK and EU. Also matters for US orgs serving EU customers
Very Common
NIST CSF
NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
US-originated but globally used as a maturity and gap assessment tool. The 6-function structure (Govern, Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover) maps well onto cloud environments and is board-friendly language.
When you'll use it: US companies, FedRAMP, maturity assessments, board reporting
Very Common
PCI DSS
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard v4.0
Mandatory for any org that stores, processes, or transmits cardholder data. 12 requirements covering network security, access control, monitoring, and testing. Cloud scope analysis (CDE) is complex and highly valued.
When you'll use it: E-commerce, fintech, retail, payment processors
Very Common
CIS Controls
Center for Internet Security Controls v8
18 prioritised security controls with cloud-specific Implementation Groups. Practical, prescriptive, and widely used as a baseline hardening standard. Strong overlap with ISO 27001 Annex A and SOC 2 criteria.
When you'll use it: Hardening assessments, gap analysis baseline, cyber insurance requirements
Sector-Specific
HIPAA
Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (US)
US federal law governing Protected Health Information (PHI). Technical, administrative, and physical safeguards. Cloud providers must sign Business Associate Agreements (BAAs). AWS, Azure, GCP all offer HIPAA-eligible services.
When you'll use it: Healthcare, health-tech, clinical research, insurance
Sector-Specific
DORA
Digital Operational Resilience Act (EU Financial Sector)
EU regulation effective Jan 2025 for financial entities and their ICT providers. Covers ICT risk management, incident reporting, resilience testing (TLPT), and third-party risk. Cloud providers to financial firms fall within scope.
When you'll use it: Banks, insurers, investment firms, fintech in EU/UK
Sector-Specific
NIS2
Network and Information Systems Directive 2 (EU)
Expands NIS1 scope to more sectors and supply chains. Stricter incident reporting (24hr initial report), management accountability, and supply chain security requirements. Sectors: energy, transport, health, digital infrastructure.
When you'll use it: Critical infrastructure, MSPs, digital service providers in EU
GRC Tools Ecosystem
03 / 06
🤖 Automated Compliance
Drata
SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA automation. Integrates with cloud APIs for evidence collection. Most popular startup choice.
Vanta
Similar to Drata. Strong for SOC 2 and ISO 27001. Good UX. Popular in UK/EU markets.
Secureframe
Multi-framework automation including HIPAA, PCI DSS. Strong vendor trust portal feature.
Sprinto
Growing platform, strong in ISO 27001 for SMEs and scaleups. Good value.
🏢 Enterprise GRC Platforms
ServiceNow GRC
Enterprise standard. Policy, risk, audit, vendor modules. Large orgs require it — learning it is highly marketable.
RSA Archer
Legacy enterprise GRC still common in large banks and regulated industries. Complex but widely deployed.
OneTrust
Privacy, third-party risk, and ESG. Industry leader for GDPR/CCPA compliance workflows and DPIA management.
LogicGate
Flexible workflow-based GRC platform. Common in US mid-market firms. Good for custom risk workflows.
☁️ Cloud-Native GRC Controls
AWS Security Hub
Aggregates findings, runs CIS benchmarks, maps to NIST and PCI controls. Essential for AWS evidence.
Azure Policy + Defender
Policy assignment, compliance reporting, and Defender for Cloud Secure Score for evidence generation.
Google Security Command Center
GCP's centralised security findings, compliance monitoring, and misconfiguration alerts.
Wiz / Prisma Cloud / Orca
CSPM tools that map cloud misconfigurations directly to framework controls. Learn at least one of these.
🛠 Practical & Open Source
Prowler
Open source CSPM for AWS, Azure, GCP. Outputs CIS/NIST/SOC 2/GDPR findings. Run it against your lab accounts.
OpenCSPM
Open source cloud security posture management. Good for understanding how CSPM tools work internally.
GRC Spreadsheets
Don't underestimate Excel/Sheets. Risk registers, control trackers, and SoAs all live in spreadsheets in the real world.
Confluence + Jira
Policy documentation and control/finding tracking. Most organisations use Atlassian. Get comfortable with both.
Control Evidence Mapping
04 / 06

What auditors ask for — and where to find it in cloud environments.

Control AreaISO 27001 / SOC 2Evidence in AWSEvidence in AzureEvidence in GCPEffort
Access ControlA.8.3 / CC6.1IAM policies, Access Analyser, CloudTrail login eventsEntra ID access reviews, PIM reports, sign-in logsIAM policy exports, Access Transparency logsMedium
MFA EnforcementA.8.5 / CC6.1IAM credential report, Config rule: mfa-enabled-for-iam-console-accessConditional Access policy export, MFA registration report2-step verification enforcement policy exportLow
Encryption at RestA.8.24 / CC6.7S3 default encryption, EBS volume encryption status, RDS configStorage Account encryption settings, Azure Disk encryption reportsGCS bucket encryption metadata, CMEK configsLow
Audit LoggingA.8.15 / CC7.2CloudTrail multi-region trail status, log file validation enabledDiagnostic settings, Activity Log retention configurationCloud Audit Logs configuration, retention settingsMedium
Vulnerability ManagementA.8.8 / CC7.1Inspector v2 findings report, patch compliance dashboardDefender for Cloud vulnerability findings, update complianceSecurity Command Center vulnerability findingsHigh
Incident ManagementA.5.24–26 / CC7.3GuardDuty findings, incident tickets, IR runbook documentationSentinel incidents, Defender alert history, IR documentationSCC findings history, incident post-mortemsHigh
Change ManagementA.8.32 / CC8.1CloudTrail API events, Config change history, IaC PR historyActivity Log change events, Azure DevOps PR recordsAudit Logs admin activity, Git history via Cloud Source ReposMedium
Data ClassificationA.5.12–13 / C1.1Macie S3 scan results, resource tagging reportsPurview data map, sensitivity label reportsCloud DLP inspection results, label taxonomyHigh
Backup & RecoveryA.8.13 / A1.2AWS Backup job reports, RDS snapshot status, DR test recordsAzure Backup reports, Site Recovery test failover recordsCloud Backup reports, snapshot policiesMedium
Supplier SecurityA.5.19–23 / CC9.2AWS Service Terms, BAA signing confirmation, vendor questionnaire recordsMicrosoft DPA / BAA confirmation, supplier questionnaire recordsGoogle Cloud DPA, CAIQ documentationHigh
GRC Career Paths
05 / 06
🔎
GRC Analyst
£35,000–£55,000 · Entry to Mid
  • Control testing and evidence collection
  • Policy documentation and maintenance
  • Audit support and RFI responses
  • Risk register updates and tracking
  • Vendor questionnaire responses (SIG, CAIQ)
📋
GRC Manager / Lead
£60,000–£90,000 · Mid to Senior
  • Own framework certifications end-to-end
  • Lead gap assessments and remediation
  • Present risk reports to leadership
  • Manage GRC platform and tooling
  • Oversee team of analysts
💼
GRC Consultant
£50,000–£90,000 · Day rate £400–700
  • ISO 27001 / SOC 2 implementation projects
  • Gap assessments across multiple clients
  • Breadth of industry exposure quickly
  • Often through a Big 4 or boutique firm
  • Strong career accelerator path
🧾
IT / Cloud Auditor
£45,000–£75,000 · Internal or External
  • Independent assessment of controls
  • Internal audit function or external firm
  • CISA certification highly valued
  • Regulated industries: banking, insurance
  • Often pathway into senior GRC or CISO roles
🔏
Data Protection Officer
£55,000–£95,000 · Specialist Track
  • GDPR accountability and advisory role
  • DPIAs, RoPA management, DSARs
  • Regulator engagement (ICO, CNIL)
  • IAPP CIPP/E certification essential
  • Cloud data governance expertise premium
🏗
Security Architect (GRC Focus)
£85,000–£130,000 · Senior Track
  • Design controls into architecture from day one
  • Compliance by design — not bolt-on
  • Works across GRC and engineering teams
  • Needs both technical and framework depth
  • Often the intersection of GRC and cloud security
Job Readiness Checklist
06a / 06
🎓 Certifications & Education
  • ★CompTIA Security+ achieved
  • ★At least one framework cert: ISO 27001 Lead Implementer or CISA
  • ✓AWS Cloud Practitioner or AZ-900 achieved (cloud fluency)
  • ✓CCSP in progress or planned (validates cloud + security depth)
  • ✓IAPP CIPP/E if targeting privacy/DPO track
📁 Portfolio Evidence
  • ★Written ISO 27001 gap assessment for a fictitious cloud org
  • ★Sample risk register with 10+ risks assessed and treated
  • ✓Cloud security policy (Information Security + Access Control)
  • ✓Sample DPIA completed for a cloud data processing scenario
  • ✓Run Prowler against a personal AWS/Azure account; mapped findings to CIS
  • ✓Signed up for a free tier on Drata or Vanta to understand the UI
🧠 Knowledge Depth
  • ★Can explain shared responsibility model for all three clouds
  • ★Can describe ISO 27001 certification journey end to end
  • ✓Can map a control to ISO 27001, SOC 2, and NIST simultaneously
  • ✓Knows GDPR breach notification process cold (72 hours)
  • ✓Understands control design vs operating effectiveness testing
  • ✓Can name three cloud-native evidence sources per control area
🎤 Interview Readiness
  • ★8 STAR-format stories prepared for behavioural questions
  • ★Can walk through an ISO 27001 implementation plan in 5 minutes
  • ✓Researched target company's compliance posture (public trust page)
  • ✓Can explain risk to a non-technical stakeholder clearly
  • ✓LinkedIn profile lists frameworks, tools, and certs prominently
  • ✓Salary benchmarks researched: Glassdoor, LinkedIn, ISACA survey
Key Resources
06b / 06
📄 Primary Source
ISO 27001:2022 Standard
Read the actual standard, not just summaries. BSI offers access. Non-negotiable for ISO roles.
📄 Primary Source
NIST CSF 2.0 (free)
Download from NIST directly at csrc.nist.gov. Free, authoritative, and referenced in almost every US-adjacent role.
🌐 Website
CSA Cloud Controls Matrix
Free CCM and CAIQ from cloudsecurityalliance.org — the definitive cloud-specific control framework. Cross-maps to ISO and SOC 2.
🌐 Website
ICO Guidance (GDPR)
ico.org.uk publishes plain-English GDPR guidance. Better than most paid training for understanding practical application.
📚 Course Platform
ISACA Learning
Official CISA, CISM, CRISC, CGEIT prep. Expensive but the best structured prep for ISACA certifications.
📚 Course Platform
IAPP Training
Essential for CIPP/E (EU privacy) and CIPM. iapp.org — join as a member for access to research and model clauses.
📚 Course Platform
A Cloud Guru / Pluralsight
Best for cloud fundamentals (AWS, Azure, GCP) with hands-on sandboxes. Bridges the GRC-to-cloud knowledge gap quickly.
📖 Book
The Privacy Engineer's Manifesto
Best practical book on operationalising privacy by design in technology systems. Pairs perfectly with GDPR work.
🎧 Podcast
Risky Business
Weekly security news and interviews. Keeps you current on breaches, regulations, and industry thinking.
🎧 Podcast
IAPP Privacy Advisor Podcast
Regulatory updates, DPA decisions, enforcement trends. Essential listening for any privacy or GRC professional.
🌐 Community
ISACA & (ISC)² Chapters
Free events, mentoring, and networking. Dundee/Edinburgh chapters in Scotland — attend even as a student member.
🛠 Free Tool
Prowler (Open Source)
Run against your AWS free tier. Outputs CIS, GDPR, SOC 2, ISO 27001 findings. Best free tool to bridge cloud and compliance.
Cloud GRC Roadmap · 2025 EditionGovern → Assess → Evidence → Certify → Apply12–18 months · All frameworks · All clouds
